title: City workers moved out to make way for apartments. Syracuse still owns the empty building 2 years later
summary: Syracuse's sale of City Hall Commons to developers Hanover Real Estate Development has stalled two years after council approval. The city vacated the historic 1869 flat-iron building to One Park Place (costing $500K annually in rent) expecting a $13.2M mixed-income conversion into 39 apartments, but the $850K sale closed in March 2024 missed its November 2025 deadline due to complex state funding requirements. The deadline has been extended another year, but council expresses frustration over continued property ownership and rental obligations.

political_signal.description: Council members expressing frustration over delayed property sale and ongoing rental obligations for temporary office space. Suggests need for better project management oversight and state funding alignment for housing initiatives.
